textByHash.9194acc3338b21c0b1b564a10687a9fe192f9328: Ah, I know them; shame about those cows. Why would they think that I could help?
textByHash.259486576063b1f7089839eb6bee1a11ccf260c5: Well, the poor young lady has such family problems; I quite feel her pain. I'd be happy to help.
textByHash.e0e4e56be211e8023fc93d3b7d803b959d637190: You seem to have one of her golden hairs on your shoulder, so I can use that...
textByHash.15b26737d9f2bf8846eefd62e8454c3840202660: Talk to me again with a ghostspeak amulet and some space in your backpack and I'll be ready to work on this little good deed. The way I plan is quite simple, really.
textByHash.50b9b5f86c74df6e6fb1265e7d8886e1ad79e6b6: I can mirror part of the unused mystical essence of the ghostspeak amulet, bind it with Alice's hair and thus create a second amulet.
textByHash.3e9ab7a9ef55ba8f6d53ee44f5c1658d623234c5: The second amulet will be useful for the purpose you desire, though it won't work for any other ghost or human other than the farmer and his wife.
textByHash.22cc8bcc0bb7ccaaed203d3a51e9bc1929574bfc: I most certainly am, but you don't have an ghostspeak amulet.
textByHash.f68298a36a1cd9feb53da079bdcfd28f89a5b1ca: I most certainly am, but you don't have space in your backpack.
textByHash.ef04a8697d8c6917fa705e196463e1ee26878289: I most certainly am; there you go.
textByHash.9e75d95b0331a40917390c777bba5f497b3971b2: Here you are; lucklily, I saved some of Alice's hair in case you were careless. Which you were.
textByHash.9039110570686bda3349069be192c7003e235787: Disgraceful! Deliver that amulet; a young lady's happiness depends upon it.
textByHash.d8534eeb14d2c172c3244e95ed7c74aede50f9f4: I don't remember; I am so old and my memory goes back only so far...
textByHash.9690d763195474ceda2db13723dd717e9018dd4f: Yes, I would love a nice hot cup of nettle tea.
textByHash.02f0b0845b68c44f54eda89cb4604ec99355b905: Yes, but it's not in my special cup! I only ever drink from my special cup!!
textByHash.e5941dd29ca402ab80051d0f77e06dce8c46cecd: I believe they grow wild in the Haunted Forest.
textByHash.072794b8e781d0e1158cf34022c7b4a93120068a: Oh no, it hasn't got milk in it. I only drink tea with milk, I'm afraid.
textByHash.08d55e5b5440597c2d011ee1f99869012919e2a2: Ah, that's better. Now, let me see... Yes, I was once a disciple of Necrovarus.
textByHash.0ca434e28e00a93b706c4c0d1f65ad657bf6688b: Yes, Velorina was once a very good friend! It has been many years since we spoke last. How is she?
textByHash.c8f183785838b87fa21cb756e9a32f02c0f1a8fa: They are all dead, then. Even Velorina. I should have done something to stop what was happening, instead of running away.
textByHash.f83dac9b35b0194fd5679e25bce09b8d1228a5f7: Do you have such a thing as a ghostspeak amulet?
textByHash.5f492b6d22483df44687efe16f0ff686a4a50aa3: Well, you'll need to find one.
textByHash.0833828f020b269c4e2423c67332f1387eb93c36: Well, that's a stroke of luck.
textByHash.9ede8590a541f213e7868f498a8aeee3826d7a9b: There is an enchantment that I can perform on such an amulet that will give it the power of command over ghosts. It will work only once, but it will enable you to command Necrovarus to let the ghosts pass on.
textByHash.5fc03cee161d86684f644276e6c6b538ade94a23: Necrovarus had a magical robe for which he must have no more use. Only these robes hold the power needed to perform the enchantment.
textByHash.2c161b80c443be2a45f519ba60a813cec53f8658: All his rituals came from a book written by an ancient sorcerer from the East called Haricanto. Bring me this strange book.
textByHash.7d913fc79cc50d15cbdab79f9e8e9327888fdf2a: I have lived here on my own for many years, but not always. When I left Port Phasmatys, I took my son with me. He grew up to be a fine boy, always in love with the sea.
textByHash.1cdca2768bbbb439bc5d73e833ab9b1f32c6d02d: He was about twelve years old when he ran away with some pirates to be a cabin boy. I never saw him again.
textByHash.7a900cf85efb1d4494dc918266b7c7beabe61dcc: If you ever see him, please give him this...and tell him that his mother still loves him.
textByHash.7da16c613ebcd82ccd1a5de498b0bd0c636903fd: Yes, he made it himself. It is a model of the very ship in which he sailed away. The paint has peeled off and it has lost its flag, but I could never throw it away.
textByHash.005efa43b05b94bbe197efd7aad51e1ac92402d2: I cannot read the strange letters of the eastern lands. I will need something to help me translate the book.
textByHash.329944682c7f36d89ca7ffc9fd36b6af537e0ee6: I never expected that you would find him - although if you do, please let me know.
textByHash.9827cbb3540370edb79ceb47537f820d61f3d95f: Oh thank you!! I will travel to see him as soon as I am able!!
textByHash.c7732b9ce4d7d84efa295fbb4af5ae521c5a7f06: The Book of Haricanto! I have no idea how you came by this, but well done!!
textByHash.4a36e7fb48db6d5200d8e759019a9b2db99ef1b0: A translation manual - yes, this should do the job.
textByHash.1554633de8cb8fc8650a33990c4f9581bf25efd1: Oh dear, that's unfortunate. You'll have to go and find another one, then.
textByHash.8bc8f798dc374de46632a330b715df6aec2aca79: I will now perform the ritual of enchantment.
textByHash.bcccbe73e1ce41aac08a5c76d97c95e04e06988c: So Necrovarus has let the ghosts go free.... You have achieved many wonderful things, my young friend.
textByHash.ee2780e37f5a07c3cb7610f4c8ccb5a682474f7a: I'm over 100 years old, you know.
textByHash.3a384ab8d39e1972105030013d3c3d7a11060711: I can't help but shed a tear about my own family. My sisters are so vile, I refuse to talk to them these days.
textByHash.18e7da896e60386109d0d8b02f6111a1b633600c: Never mind; we've reunited a happy family of farmers; that's enough for me.
